descriptionEn: This course introduces the philosophy, methods and principles of Slow Design, using examples of existing Slow Design products as well as new Slow Design concepts, and to inspire students to apply the methods and principles to their creative design process.
descriptionZh: 本課程利用現有的「慢設計」產品與「慢設計」概念介紹「慢設計」之設計哲學、方法與準則，並藉以啟發學生將其運用於創新媒體設計或動畫影像設計領域。

descriptionEn: This course introduces recent trends in art and performance theory while also focusing on the practical application of these concepts. During the semester, students generate their original self-comprehension of their own creation or performance about an issue, event, person, or historical topic of their own choosing. They construct the script using a variety of nonfiction sources including autobiographies, diaries, textbooks, interviews, and newspaper articles. This course challenges students to initiate an intuitive search of him/her-self and to consider the essence of creation as they embody their thoughts, feelings, and daily life events. Students learn basic interdisciplinary fields, research methodologies and intuitive ways of self-interpretation in the class, encouraging them to critically examine the power dynamics implicit and impact of introspection in all extraneous and internal factors (such as social and psychological exchanges). The semester ends with each student representing a full creation of costume or animation (for both sections of fashion design and communications design) and a group public exhibition which integrates all the students’ final works.
descriptionZh: 希望藉由本課程，幫助研究生能以一個初學者的心態和衝動重新去觸摸自我，藉由對自己影響深遠的設計師、藝術家、風格、派別、多元創作領域加以深入研究探討，分析自身的感動與其關連性。透過課堂評圖與互動，幫助學生訓練對自我作品認知的文字與語言陳述，以及創作過程的紀錄，培養學生個人對人文創作的敏銳度以及對事物的多重、複合感受，包含直覺性與思考性等不同程度上的反省與詮釋，建立日後在創作論文寫作與作品發想、實驗、階段完成、修正、再實驗、再調整等過程之基礎。

descriptionEn: Through research and implementation of the content of the course, the interactive script includes: the interaction of video games, board games, video works and many other fields is the principle of script writing.
descriptionZh: 藉由研究與實作並重的課程內容，認識互動式劇本包含：電子遊戲、桌遊、影像作品等諸多領域的互動是劇本編寫原則。

descriptionEn: This course introduces recent trends in art and performance theory while also focusing on the practical application of these concepts. During the semester, students generate their original self-comprehension of their own creation or performance about an issue, event, person, or historical topic of their own choosing. They construct the script using a variety of nonfiction sources including autobiographies, diaries, textbooks, interviews, and newspaper articles. This course challenges students to initiate an intuitive search of him/her-self and to consider the essence of creation as they embody their thoughts, feelings, and daily life events. Students learn basic interdisciplinary fields, research methodologies and intuitive ways of self-interpretation in the class, encouraging them to critically examine the power dynamics implicit and impact of introspection in all extraneous and internal factors (such as social and psychological exchanges). The semester ends with each student representing a full creation of costume or animation (for both sections of fashion design and communications design) and a group public exhibition which integrates all the students’ final works.
descriptionZh: 希望藉由本課程，幫助研究生能以一個初學者的心態和衝動重新去觸摸自我，藉由對自己影響深遠的設計師、藝術家、風格、派別、多元創作領域加以深入研究探討，分析自身的感動與其關連性。透過課堂評圖與互動，幫助學生訓練對自我作品認知的文字與語言陳述，以及創作過程的紀錄，培養學生個人對人文創作的敏銳度以及對事物的多重、複合感受，包含直覺性與思考性等不同程度上的反省與詮釋，建立日後在創作論文寫作與作品發想、實驗、階段完成、修正、再實驗、再調整等過程之基礎。

descriptionEn: Maps, cartography, and visual arts have long been related disciplines, each offering different means and methods to identify and depict space, while implicitly assuming the importance of doing so. The course content explores how art travelers have historically followed the trajectories of political, economic, and cultural expansion. As an increasing number of artists use maps as a medium of expression and seek to re-engage with visual representation, this course guides students to recognize alternative forms of creative practice through the abstraction of cartography and the communicative power of mapping strategies.
descriptionZh: 地圖、製圖和視覺藝術長期以來一直是相關學科，每個學科都提供了不同的手段和方法來識別和描繪空間，同時隱含地假設這樣做的重要性。課程內容探討藝術旅行者如何歷來追隨政治、經濟和文化的擴張軌跡。隨著越來越多藝術家將地圖作為一種表達媒介，並尋求重新參與視覺表現，本課程引導學生藉由製圖學的抽象性與映射策略的交流能力，重新認識創作的其他可能形式。

titleEn: Seminar in Media and Visual Culture
descriptionEn: How do we develop a media theory that's adequate to account for all the forms of media and cross-mediation that we experience today? This seminar will provide an overview of contemporary theories of media, communication, semiotics, and visual culture to allow for an integrative viewpoint. Our themes will include the cultural encoding of the material forms media, mediation and cultural transmission, the rhetoric of communication and information technologies, and the institutional and social contexts of media and the visual arts. Readings will include seminal works in semiotics and discourse analysis, rhetoric, cultural theory, the sociology of media and technology, post-postmodern cultural theory, and contemporary theory in the visual arts. We will look closely at the work of Regis Debray and the model of mediology, which has attempted to describe a metatheory of communication and media. We will expand the application of mediology to contemporary media and visual culture. Students will be expected to create the seminar in real time through readings, discussion, proposal of cases and examples for study. Specific weekly topics for discussion and examples for applied theory will depend on seminar participants. Grading and weekly seminar assignments will be based on student presentations, short applied theory essays, and a final project involving the application of a theoretical mode to a contemporary case.
descriptionZh: 隨著科技的演進，載負視覺符號的媒介形式，由早先的洞穴圖像、繪畫，攝影，到影音動態的電影、電視、動畫，再到近期數位技術驅動的電腦多媒體、虛擬互動網路等等，其次躍進式的突變，再再的衝擊了人類的認知體驗，也顛覆了訊息傳達的製碼/解碼的格律。針對這個轉變，歷時性的檢視近代社會學、經濟學、藝術美學、人類學、與文化研究等領域，有關視覺構成與媒介形式的經典論著來思辯：各種視覺媒介構成的美學特質、與其訊息傳達的法則，以及它們在訊息傳達過程中，涉及的真實/虛擬真實/超真實，還有集體價值意識的模塑、文化體驗建構等方面的議題。目的在於，讓學生在從事視覺經驗批評論辯，或創意實作時，能具備多面向的、哲思性的省思，並產生顛覆性的透視。

titleZh: 跨領域思考方式與創作
descriptionEn: In this course, students explore the art of adaptation and inter-disciplinary process. Students experiment with different techniques of translating ideas from one artistic medium to another based on their own area of creative interest and expertise. Over the course of the semester, students will work both individually and in groups to create original costume, set, video, and/or graphic designs based on a given theme or source material through the guest foreign professors instruction and lecture. This course is designed to stimulate students' creative process and open new interdisciplinary collaborative opportunities.
descriptionZh: 本課程旨在讓學生探索藝術作品的內在認知過程，以及對藝術的接受與適應。同時，學生可藉由各自不同的背景、原創性與專業領域，透過多元創作媒介，體驗不同的創作技巧與藝術語彙的轉譯。

descriptionEn: The course aims to inculcate students' independent research skills by providing professional guest lecturers from the industry to share the work experience.
descriptionZh: 本課程旨在以業師專業實務經驗分享，培育學生專題研究實務能力。

descriptionEn: The goal of this course is to teach students to write critical, scholarly essays. The course covers academic disciplines including social sciences and behavioral studies while introducing students to a variety of research methodologies and practices.
descriptionZh: 除了指導藝術設計創作之外，此課程將培育同學對學術研究有正確的認知，並熟悉論文寫作的法則，內容包括研究精神的闡釋、研究方法的介紹、論文格式的講授。

descriptionEn:
This course centers on the question of what “creation” and “creative practice” mean to both the individual and the collective. It further examines the distinction and relationship between creation and creativity. The first phase begins with the instructor’s and students’ own creative experiences, exploring how artworks reflect personal narratives and social contexts. It then extends to analyses of contemporary media techniques through the lenses of aesthetics and phenomenology.

The second phase focuses on the practice and theory of visual composition, investigating how framing and structure shape visual language and narrative expression. The third phase delves into the materiality and mediality of media, examining how different media forms affect perception, meaning, and creative processes.

Emphasizing humanistic inquiry, artistic experimentation, and innovative thinking, the course encourages interdisciplinary collaboration and participation. Throughout the semester, students will engage in activities organized by the College of Design—such as semester reviews, exhibitions, international symposia, and intensive cross-disciplinary workshops—to cultivate an integrated approach to creativity and research.
descriptionZh:
本課程以探討「創作」之於個人與集體的意義為核心問題，進一步思考「創造」與「創作」之間的差異與關係。課程初期將從授課者與學生的創作經驗出發，檢視作品如何反映個體生命經驗與時代脈絡，並延伸至對當代媒材技法的分析，從美學與現象學的角度展開辯證。

第二階段著重於影像構圖的實踐與理論，探討構圖如何形成視覺語言與敘事結構。第三階段則聚焦於媒材本身的特性與「媒體性」（mediality）的探討，思考不同媒介如何影響感知、意義與創造行為。

課程以人文關懷、藝術實踐與創新思維為核心，並鼓勵學生跨域合作與參與。全學期將配合設計學院舉辦之跨領域課程與學術交流活動，如學期成果展、國際研討會、展覽與密集式國際工作營等，培養學生整合創意與研究的能力。

titleEn: The Media History on Analytical and Critical Perspective
descriptionEn: The goal of this course is to guide students to complete a complete interactive work, including video or installation works.
descriptionZh: 本課程目標在引導同學完成一個完整的互動作品，方向包括影像類或是裝置類作品。

descriptionEn: This course explores the influence of visual imagery in our contemporary media information-based society and the convergence of contemporary cultural studies and interdisciplinary arts. Lacan‘s writings serve as a primary source material for the discussion of a variety of forms of visual representation (photography, drawing, film, advertisements, etc.) and the relationship between media and contemporary culture and society.
descriptionZh: 探討在當代資訊媒體社會裏，影像所展現其無遠弗屆的影響力，以及當代文化學科跨領域的匯聚所在。它不止於物質平面，更是多維度的象徵物；不僅被閱讀，也是論述的生產，甚至是論述如何被生產的介面。本課程特別強調記號學與拉岡（Lacan）精神分析理論的交叉運用，在以各種影像生產（攝影、繪畫、電影、廣告等等）為參照下，研讀相關的理論文本，研討影像的構成與當代社會及文化理論的內在聯繫。

descriptionEn: To cultivate students' basic talents for the use of various information media in the future, to train students to understand the procedures of various information channels and media interfaces, and to thoroughly understand the principles and related knowledge of this ecosystem. Let students use their thinking to understand the real essence.
descriptionZh: 培養學生為之後各種訊息媒介利用的基礎人才、訓練學生了解各種訊息管道與媒體介面的程序、使徹底了解此生態圈的、原理及相關知識。讓同學活用思維，使其了解真正的本質面。

descriptionEn: Through research and implementation of the content of the course, the interactive script includes: the interaction of video games, board games, video works and many other fields is the principle of script writing.
descriptionZh: 藉由研究與實作並重的課程內容，認識互動式劇本包含：電子遊戲、桌遊、影像作品等諸多領域的互動是劇本編寫原則。

descriptionEn: The goal of this course is to teach students to write critical, scholarly essays. The course covers academic disciplines including social sciences and behavioral studies, while introducing students to a variety of research methodologies and practices.
descriptionZh: 本課程旨在培育同學對學術研究有正確的認知，並熟悉論文寫作的法則，內容包括研究精神的闡釋、研究方法的介紹、論文格式的講授。

descriptionEn: This semester's course will focus on how "digital media" can become an effective means of communication, and explore how digital creations can be transformed into tangible media forms. Students will engage in extensive hands-on practice, interacting with different audiences, contexts, and settings, in order to understand various approaches and methods in design. The course will incorporate exhibition visits, case studies, and specialized lectures, aiming to provide a comprehensive learning experience that integrates theory with practice, helping students deepen their thinking and apply their creative ideas in the field of digital media creation.
descriptionZh: 本學期的課程將圍繞「數位媒體」如何成為一種有效的傳達語言，並探索如何將數位創作轉化為具體的媒介形式。學生將透過大量的實際練習，與不同對象、情境和基地進行互動對話，從中理解設計的多元途徑與方法。課程內容將結合展覽參觀、案例研究以及專題講座，旨在提供理論與實踐相結合的全面學習體驗，幫助學生在數位媒體創作領域中深入思考並實踐其創意構想。
